The key point to emphasize here is that cortisol has significant effects on metabolism which can indirectly influence cardiovascular function by affecting electrolyte balance and fluid homeostasis, thereby contributing to increased blood volume and subsequently higher blood pressure through its interaction with other systems including the renin-angiotensin system. This makes it more effective than the alternatives listed for directly enhancing vascular smooth muscle tone via hormonal mechanisms alone.
